summary: Clear soundboard recording with a touch of hiss captures a high‑energy 1966 show led by Pigpen, highlighted by standout Smokestack Lightning, Cream Puff War and an epic Midnight Hour.
review: The SBD source is generally clear though it suffers from slight background hiss, occasional pops and a cut‑off in the middle of “Dancing in the Streets”. Musically the concert is a Pigpen‑heavy powerhouse – tight playing, frantic up‑tempo numbers and several first‑time or rare performances that reviewers call “amazing”, especially Smokestack Lightning into King Bee, Cream Puff War and an 18‑minute Midnight Hour jam.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for the performance (tight playing, many standout jams) combined with a mostly clean soundboard recording; minor audio defects keep it from an “excellent” rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Slight background hiss and occasional snap/crackle/pops, but overall clear. No radio interruptions. The version of "Dancing in the Streets" is cut off about eight minutes into Jerry's solo.

standout_songs: Beat It On Down the Line
show_quality.setlist_flow: A fast‑paced, Pigpen‑driven set with many up‑tempo numbers; first‑time debut of Smokestack Lightning leading into a bluesy King Bee, followed by extended jams that keep the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as crisp and front‑and‑center with stellar guitar tone; leads several memorable jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Summers humorously mentioned on drums, but no substantive evaluation of drumming quality.
